qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded PBKDF2 iteration count enables denial of service through CPU exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns unbounded PBKDF2 iteration counts in core/pbkdf2.js, not missing point-on-curve validation in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey() or dhJavaEc() ECDH output leakage.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution in JSON object merging allows privilege escalation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns object merging in core/convenience.js _add(), not ECC public-key point validation in core/ecc.js.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Timing side-channel in MAC tag verification allows authentication bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns non-constant-time MAC tag comparison in bitArray.equal() and authenticated modes, not off-curve ECDH public-key acceptance in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey().

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Use of Math.random() in PRNG reseed compromises cryptographic security
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns Math.random() use in core/random.js reseeding; its ECC mention is general key generation impact, not missing point-on-curve validation or dhJavaEc() raw-coordinate oracle behavior.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: missing point-on-curve validation in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey(), acceptance of crafted off-curve ECDH public keys, or dhJavaEc() returning a raw scalar-multiplication x-coordinate oracle.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2w8x-224x-785m describes improper cryptographic verification in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey() and dhJavaEc() ECDH behavior in core/ecc.js.
The four final qualifying findings are PBKDF2 iteration DoS, prototype pollution in convenience.js object merging, MAC verification timing behavior, and weak PRNG reseeding with Math.random().

Searches across final finding folders and session logs found no advisory-aligned references to publicKey(), dhJavaEc(), off-curve public keys, missing point-on-curve validation, or the advisory source-to-sink path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
